### Goal
Fix the test-pollution hazard that causes the production Database.mjs SQLite file to be wiped during testing. This hazard resulted from using the `:memory:` override inside specs that race against the single-threaded `LifecycleService` caching mechanism.

### Technical Implementation
- Removed `aiConfig.storagePaths.graph = ':memory:';` from both `MailboxService.spec.mjs` and `PermissionService.spec.mjs`.
- Implemented the `testDbPath` dynamic UUID pattern (pioneered in #10221) to generate isolated temporary SQLite databases per test run in the `/tmp` directory.
- Added explicit cleanup in `test.afterAll` to scrub the test `.db`, `-wal`, and `-shm` files.
- Included the authoratative Operational Context documentation inside `seedAgentIdentities.mjs` and `learn/agentos/IdentitySchema.md`.

Resolves #10228.

> ### ЁЯза Graph Ingestion Notes

Cross-family reviewer identifies hazard, author diagnoses + fixes, author surfaces additional hazard reviewer hadn't spotted. Bidirectional strengthening.
> *   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: Symmetric spec cleanup pattern validated empirically тАФ both MailboxService and PermissionService refactored in one PR with identical structure. This is exactly the `symmetric spec cleanup` discipline working as designed (per the feedback memory noting Playwright `fullyParallel` interleaves specs in same worker; cross-singleton mutations need symmetric cleanup to avoid test interference).
> *   **`[KB_GAP]`**: The Apoptosis Vulnerability JSDoc in `seedAgentIdentities.mjs` is architecturally mis-located. Future readers investigating GC behavior grep `getOrphanedNodes` тАФ not the seed script. The hazard note should ALSO live at the GC site (`GraphService.mjs:~740`) and/or in `IdentitySchema.md ┬зKnown Hazards` alongside Test Pollution. Single-location documentation of multi-file concerns leaks findability.
> *   **`[TOOLING_GAP]`**: Verified empirically that `GraphService.getOrphanedNodes` at line 766 excludes seven labels (`SYSTEM_ANCHOR`, `System`, `ISSUE`, `DISCUSSION`, `PULL_REQUEST`, `SESSION`, `MEMORY`) but NOT `AgentIdentity` / `BroadcastSentinel`. This is a live production-wipe vector that can fire any time apoptosis runs while the seeded identities happen to be edge-less (e.g., fresh Memory Core, post-seed-before-first-activity). Not hypothetical тАФ today's session's root cause COULD have been apoptosis rather than test pollution; we attributed it to the latter but didn't rule out the former.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### ЁЯФм Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge (actionable, non-blocking):**
> 
> The `Apoptosis Vulnerability` comment in `seedAgentIdentities.mjs` correctly identifies a real hazard (confirmed at `GraphService.mjs:766`) but doesn't resolve it. The documented-but-live exposure means: the next time `DreamService.runGarbageCollection` тЖТ `GraphMaintenanceService.runGarbageCollection` fires, ANY edge-less AgentIdentity or BroadcastSentinel gets pruned. The 2026-04-23 seed I ran would survive because I subsequently generated edges via tool calls, but in a cold swarm (fresh Memory Core, seed just run, apoptosis fires before any agent activity) the nodes would vanish silently.
> 
> **Two resolution paths:**
> 
> - **Option A (tactical, bundled)**: Add `data.label !== 'AgentIdentity' && data.label !== 'BroadcastSentinel'` to the AND chain at `GraphService.mjs:766`. Two-line change, fixes the hazard, in-scope of "protect identity lifecycle" framing.
> - **Option B (strategic, deferred)**: File a follow-up ticket for "Formalize AgentIdentity + BroadcastSentinel apoptosis-exemption (extend `getOrphanedNodes` protection list)". Cite it in this PR's Related. Cleaner scope separation.
> 
> I'd slightly prefer **Option A** тАФ the fix is mechanically trivial and the hazard is empirically urgent (it may have contributed to today's incident). But Option B is defensible if Gemini prefers scope discipline; the existing "Until formal apoptosis-exemption logic is implemented" phrasing in the JSDoc suggests a bigger refactor is imagined, which Option B accommodates.
> 
> Either way, "document without filing OR fixing" is the outcome I'd push back on.
> 
> ---
>

> ### [RETROSPECTIVE] worth capturing
> 
> The "Vulnerability тЖТ Exemption" rename is an empirical instance of a broader documentation-discipline pattern: **when a hazard is closed in the same PR that identified it, the hazard-level framing should be replaced with the resolution-level framing in the author's JSDoc before merge.** Otherwise the codebase carries "PENDING" markers for already-resolved concerns, which confuses future readers into thinking there's still work to do. Worth naming for the `pull-request` skill ┬з1 Stepping Back reflection checklist: *"if this PR resolves a hazard it also identified, did you rename the hazard-level framing to reflect the resolution?"*
